claim: Provider-native tools (Anthropic computer use, web search, memory) are model-optimized and more reliable than hand-rolled generic function calls; they should be preferred via tools primitives in SDK.
actionable: Use provider-native tools (Anthropic computer_use, web_search, memory) instead of writing custom tool wrappers; they are tuned for the model.
source_title: I Let an AI Control My Browser to Play Tic-Tac-Toe - LangChainJS Tutorials

claim: Agent computer use (UI automation) differs fundamentally from deterministic WebDriver automation; models see screenshots and reason about clicks, not parsing brittle DOM selectors.
actionable: For AI-driven browser automation, expect model-based UI reasoning (screenshot -> click) not selector-based; this is more robust to UI changes but slower.
source_title: I Let an AI Control My Browser to Play Tic-Tac-Toe - LangChainJS Tutorials

claim: Agent memory tools can track mistakes across game iterations; agents improve performance by reviewing prior failures in memory before subsequent attempts.
actionable: For repeated tasks, integrate memory tools so agents can update notes on failures and strategies; they become progressively better over multiple runs.
source_title: I Let an AI Control My Browser to Play Tic-Tac-Toe - LangChainJS Tutorials
